payload.audience: application developers using dali-ui public APIs

content_quality.default_object_model: Use Dali::Ui::View rather than Dali::Actor for app-facing object examples.
content_quality.guide_assumption: The reader is using dali-ui to build applications.

code_verification: receiver method exists on the receiver type
static methods are called statically and member methods are called on valid instances
assignment target is compatible with the method return type
argument count, argument type, and overload shape match the public API
enum owner and enum value both exist
property constants use the correct owner and value type
signal callback signatures match the public signal typedef
code blocks do not rely on inferred namespaces or overloads from naming alone
draft_requirements: Write enough practical detail for application developers to use the feature in a dali-ui app.
Ground natural-language behavior claims in the actual public headers, implementation files, samples, or UTCs when the context pack is not enough.
Before writing examples, inspect the referenced public headers and relevant sample files when needed.
Prefer examples copied or adapted from existing samples when they match the feature; otherwise keep examples small and verify every API use.
After drafting, review the completed Markdown line by line against repository evidence and fix inaccurate explanations or code before returning.
repair_requirements: Repair only the problematic spans and the smallest surrounding context needed; do not rewrite the whole document.
For each listed issue, use the repository evidence and context pack to verify the corrected wording or code before returning.
If an issue reveals the same API misuse nearby, fix the nearby equivalent misuse too; do not make unrelated style rewrites.
After repair, re-check the edited lines against public headers, implementation files, samples, or UTCs when needed.

rules: Use dali-ui symbols as the app-facing API surface.
Use dali-core and dali-adaptor symbols only as setup/background unless the usage pattern explicitly marks them preferred.
Prefer View-based examples over raw Actor object-model examples.

usage_patterns.intent: Create and configure the primary object using public dali-ui APIs.
usage_patterns.usage_note: Prefer typed constructors and setters when available.

usage_patterns.intent: React to user or resource events through public signals.
usage_patterns.usage_note: Use signal connection APIs only with their owning trait or class.

rendered_prompt:
You are reviewing an existing DALi dali-ui application developer guide draft.
This is Phase 2.3.5: source-grounded prose review.

Primary goal:
- Improve natural-language accuracy and guide flow using repository evidence.
- Preserve the document's useful structure and examples whenever possible.
- Do not perform a full rewrite.
- Keep the revised user-facing guide in en only.
- Remove Korean, Japanese, Chinese, or other non-English prose from the revised guide.

Feature target:
- Feature: Label (label)
- Category: views-components
- Guide profile: dali-ui-app
- Audience: application developers using dali-ui public APIs

Review rules:
- Review every natural-language paragraph line by line against public headers, implementation files, samples, and UTCs when needed.
- Accuracy is the first priority. Flow is the second priority.
- Edit the smallest unit needed: word, sentence, paragraph, or section-local addition/removal.
- If a word-level change is enough, change only the word.
- If a sentence-level change is enough, change only the sentence.
- If an entire paragraph is based on a wrong premise, revise, add, or remove that paragraph.
- Preserve code blocks when possible.
- If prose can be corrected to match an already accurate code block, prefer changing prose.
- If the code block itself makes the prose inaccurate or breaks the guide flow, rewrite only the smallest necessary part of that code block and verify it against public headers/source.
- Do not invent APIs.
- Do not add generic filler just to make the document longer.
- Do not change section structure unless prose_review.allow_section_restructure is true or the current prose is clearly misleading.
- Prefer localized edits when prose_review.localized_edits_only is true.
- Preserve code blocks especially when prose_review.preserve_code_blocks is true.
- Prefer Dali::Ui::View, typed setters, fluent chaining, and view-tree idioms for app-facing guidance.
- Treat dali-core and dali-adaptor APIs as reference/context unless the feature explicitly requires them.

Context summary:
{
  "content_quality": {
    "overview_style": "very_short",
    "require_table_of_contents": true,
    "require_rich_api_examples": true,
    "require_backticks_for_api_names": true,
    "topic_sections_must_be_feature_specific": true,
    "avoid_mechanical_api_listing": true,
    "default_object_model": "Use Dali::Ui::View rather than Dali::Actor for app-facing object examples.",
    "guide_assumption": "The reader is using dali-ui to build applications."
  },
  "prose_review": {
    "enabled": true,
    "apply_to": "all",
    "preserve_code_blocks": true,
    "localized_edits_only": true,
    "allow_section_restructure": false,
    "output_review_report": true,
    "fail_open_on_parse_error": true

---
title: Label
sidebar_label: Label
category: views-components
---

# Label

`Dali::Ui::Label` is the dali-ui view for displaying non-editable text in an application view tree.

## Table of Contents

- [Create a Label](#create-a-label)
- [Choose Font and Typographic Style](#choose-font-and-typographic-style)
- [Control Scale, Line Height, and Direction](#control-scale-line-height-and-direction)
- [Handle Anchor Clicks](#handle-anchor-clicks)
- [Use Asynchronous Rendering and Render Scale](#use-asynchronous-rendering-and-render-scale)
- [Read Label State](#read-label-state)

## Create a Label

Use `Dali::Ui::Label::New` to create the text view. The overload `Dali::Ui::Label::New(const Dali::String&)` sets the initial text during construction, so it is the most direct choice for application UI labels.

```cpp
#include <dali-ui-foundation/dali-ui-foundation.h>

using namespace Dali;
using namespace Dali::Ui;

Dali::Ui::View CreateTitle()
{
  Dali::Ui::Label title =
    Dali::Ui::Label::New("Account settings")
      .SetFontFamily("SamsungOneUI_700")
      .SetFontSize(32.0f)
      .SetFontWeight(Text::FontWeight::BOLD)
      .SetHorizontalTextAlignment(Text::Alignment::CENTER);

  return title;
}
```

`Dali::Ui::Label` is a `Dali::Ui::View`, so application code can return it, store it, and add it to view-based layouts as part of the dali-ui view tree.

## Choose Font and Typographic Style

A label exposes typed setters for common font selection. Use `SetFontFamily`, `SetFontSize`, `SetFontWeight`, `SetFontWidth`, and `SetFontSlant` when the app needs a specific typographic treatment.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::Label CreateEmphasisLabel(const Dali::String& text)
{
  return Dali::Ui::Label::New(text)
    .SetFontFamily("SamsungOneUI_400")
    .SetFontSize(22.0f)
    .SetFontWeight(Text::FontWeight::SEMI_BOLD)
    .SetFontWidth(Text::FontWidth::NORMAL)
    .SetFontSlant(Text::FontSlant::ITALIC);
}
```

For variable fonts, `SetFontVariation` replaces the current font variation axes. The string form accepts OpenType axis tags such as `wght` and `wdth`.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::Label CreateVariableFontLabel()
{
  return Dali::Ui::Label::New("Variable weight")
    .SetFontFamily("SamsungOneUI_400")
    .SetFontSize(28.0f)
    .SetFontVariation("wght=600,wdth=90");
}
```

If you already have explicit axes, pass a `Dali::Vector<Text::FontVariationAxis>` to `SetFontVariation`.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::Label CreateAxisBasedLabel()
{
  Dali::Vector<Text::FontVariationAxis> axes;
  axes.PushBack(Text::FontVariationAxis("wght", 700.0f));
  axes.PushBack(Text::FontVariationAxis("wdth", 100.0f));

  return Dali::Ui::Label::New("Axis configured")
    .SetFontSize(26.0f)
    .SetFontVariation(axes);
}
```

## Control Scale, Line Height, and Direction

`SetFontSizeScale` multiplies the configured font size before text layout and rendering. Use `SetMinimumFontSizeScale` and `SetMaximumFontSizeScale` to keep app text within a controlled accessibility range.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::Label CreateScaledLabel()
{
  return Dali::Ui::Label::New("Readable label")
    .SetFontSize(20.0f)
    .SetFontSizeScale(1.15f)
    .SetMinimumFontSizeScale(0.9f)
    .SetMaximumFontSizeScale(1.4f);
}
```

Use `SetLineHeight` for multi-line text rhythm. The meaning of the line height value depends on `SetLineHeightMode`: `Text::LineHeightMode::RELATIVE` treats the value as a multiplier, while `Text::LineHeightMode::ABSOLUTE` treats it as a pixel value.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::Label CreateBodyLabel()
{
  return Dali::Ui::Label::New("A short paragraph of app text.")
    .SetFontSize(18.0f)
    .SetLineHeight(1.4f)
    .SetLineHeightMode(Text::LineHeightMode::RELATIVE)
    .SetHorizontalTextAlignment(Text::Alignment::START);
}
```

For bidirectional or locale-sensitive text, `SetLayoutDirectionMode` controls how the label resolves text direction.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::Label CreateLocaleDirectedLabel(const Dali::String& text)
{
  return Dali::Ui::Label::New(text)
    .SetFontSize(20.0f)
    .SetLayoutDirectionMode(Text::LayoutDirectionMode::LOCALE);
}
```

## Handle Anchor Clicks

`AnchorClickedSignal` is emitted when an anchor in label text is clicked. The callback receives the source `Dali::Ui::View` and the clicked anchor `href`.

```cpp
class SettingsPage : public Dali::ConnectionTracker
{
public:
  Dali::Ui::Label CreateTermsLabel()
  {
    Dali::Ui::Label label =
      Dali::Ui::Label::New("Terms and privacy")
        .SetFontSize(18.0f)
        .SetAnchorColor(Dali::Ui::UiColor(0x0066CC))
        .SetAnchorClickedColor(Dali::Ui::UiColor(0x663399));

    label.AnchorClickedSignal().Connect(this, &SettingsPage::OnAnchorClicked);
    return label;
  }

private:
  void OnAnchorClicked(Dali::Ui::View view, const Dali::String& href)
  {
    Dali::Ui::Label label = Dali::Ui::Label::DownCast(view);
    if(label)
    {
      const Dali::String text = label.GetText();
      const Dali::Ui::UiColor clickedColor = label.GetAnchorClickedColor();
    }
  }
};
```

Use `SetAnchorColor` for the normal anchor color and `SetAnchorClickedColor` for the clicked state color. The matching read APIs are `GetAnchorColor` and `GetAnchorClickedColor`.

## Use Asynchronous Rendering and Render Scale

`SetAsyncRendering(true)` lets the label request asynchronous text rendering during relayout when needed. `SetRenderScale` controls the scale used to rasterize glyphs for rendering quality; it is valid when asynchronous rendering is enabled and must be at least `1.0f`.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::Label CreateLargeScaledLabel()
{
  return Dali::Ui::Label::New("Featured")
    .SetFontFamily("SamsungOneUI_700")
    .SetFontSize(48.0f)
    .SetAsyncRendering(true)
    .SetRenderScale(1.5f);
}
```

Use `GetAsyncLineCount` after an asynchronous result has completed to read the line count from the most recent async result.

```cpp
void InspectAsyncLabel(const Dali::Ui::Label& label)
{
  const int asyncLines = label.GetAsyncLineCount();
  const float renderScale = label.GetRenderScale();
}
```

For visual effects where glyph shapes should cut through the rendered content, enable cutout rendering with `SetCutoutEnabled`.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::Label CreateCutoutLabel()
{
  return Dali::Ui::Label::New("CUTOUT")
    .SetFontSize(56.0f)
    .SetFontWeight(Text::FontWeight::BLACK)
    .SetCutoutEnabled(true);
}
```

## Read Label State

Getter methods let application code inspect the text configuration currently held by a label. This is useful when synchronizing UI controls with label state.

```cpp
struct LabelTextState
{
  Dali::String fontFamily;
  float fontSize;
  float fontSizeScale;
  float adjustedFontSizeScale;
  Text::FontWeight fontWeight;
  Text::FontWidth fontWidth;
  Text::FontSlant fontSlant;
};

LabelTextState ReadTextState(const Dali::Ui::Label& label)
{
  LabelTextState state;
  state.fontFamily = label.GetFontFamily();
  state.fontSize = label.GetFontSize();
  state.fontSizeScale = label.GetFontSizeScale();
  state.adjustedFontSizeScale = label.GetAdjustedFontSizeScale();
  state.fontWeight = label.GetFontWeight();
  state.fontWidth = label.GetFontWidth();
  state.fontSlant = label.GetFontSlant();
  return state;
}
```

Layout-related getters mirror the typed setters used for configuration.

```cpp
void ReadLayoutState(Dali::Ui::Label label)
{
  const Text::Alignment horizontal = label.GetHorizontalTextAlignment();
  const Text::Alignment vertical = label.GetVerticalTextAlignment();
  const Text::LayoutDirectionMode direction = label.GetLayoutDirectionMode();
  const Text::LineHeightMode lineHeightMode = label.GetLineHeightMode();
  const float lineHeight = label.GetLineHeight();
  const int currentLineCount = label.GetLineCount();
}
```

`Dali::Ui::Label::Property` contains the property constants owned by the label, including `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::TEXT`, `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::FONT_SIZE`, `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::TEXT_COLOR`, `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::ANCHOR_COLOR`, `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::ASYNC_RENDERING`, and `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::RENDER_SCALE`. Prefer the typed `Dali::Ui::Label` setters and getters in application code when they cover the same setting.
